name: Crispy Quail Salad
recipeIngredient: 1 Tablespoon Chinese Chile Paste with Garlic
1/4 Cup Dry White Wine or Water
1 Cup Julienned Red Bell Pepper (seeded and stemmed)
recipeInstructions:
FOR THE QUAIL:
1. In a blender, combine hoisin sauce, soy sauce, oyster sauce, chile paste, garlic, ginger, and wine. Pulse to make a smooth mixture. Whisk together and add quail; toss to coat evenly. Marinate in the refrigerator for at least 2 hours.
2. Heat oil in a deep pan to 350°F (use a thermometer). Combine flour, cayenne, 5 spice powder, and salt on a plate.
3. Remove quail from marinade and wipe off excess; lightly coat with the flour mixture. Deep fry quail in batches until golden brown and crispy (6-8 minutes). Check at the leg joint to ensure the quail is cooked through. Keep quail warm in a low oven, and make sure oil returns to 350°F before adding new pieces.
4. Toss peppers, jicama, scallions, and carrots with about 1/3 cup of the vinaigrette and arrange attractively on plates. Top with a warm quail portion and serve immediately garnished with cilantro sprigs. Yield: 6 servings
FOR THE VINAIGRETTE:
1. Sauté shallots in olive oil until soft but not brown. Set aside to cool.
2. Whisk together lemon juice, honey (or to taste), salt, and pepper.
3. Store refrigerated up to 5 days. Yield: approximately 1 cup
